signature.instructions:
Extract the treatment(s) applicable to THIS sample — the drug(s),
compound(s), genetic perturbation(s), exposure(s), or clinical /
experimental procedure(s) applied to this particular sample.

Return the intervention name(s) as they appear in the metadata. When
more than one applies to this sample, join with "; " in the order
they appear. For a control / vehicle / untreated arm, return the
exact control substring (e.g. "DMSO", "shRNA_Ctrl", "non-silencing
vector", "0nM dasatinib"). If no intervention applies, output
exactly: Not Specified.

Treatments include:
  - Drugs and small-molecule compounds, recognised by name or by
    characteristic suffix: -mab (trastuzumab, rituximab), -nib
    (imatinib, dasatinib, bortezomib), -mycin (rapamycin, doxycycline),
    -ciclib, -statin, -tinib, -parib, -lisib, -zumab. Also named
    chemotherapeutics (cisplatin, doxorubicin, 5-FU, paclitaxel,
    temozolomide, methotrexate), steroids (dexamethasone, prednisone),
    cytokines (IL-2, TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma), growth factors (EGF, FGF),
    hormones (estradiol, tamoxifen, insulin), antibiotics (puromycin,
    G418, hygromycin) used as experimental reagents.
  - Dose / concentration + drug patterns: keep the full substring with
    the concentration attached ("100 nM dasatinib", "10 uM MG132",
    "0nM dasatinib" as control, "1 ug/mL doxycycline", "5 Gy
    irradiation"). A zero or vehicle concentration of a named drug is
    still a valid Treatment span (it denotes the control arm).
  - Vehicles / solvents as explicit control arms: DMSO, PBS, saline,
    ethanol, water, vehicle, mock, untreated, sham.
  - Genetic perturbations: shRNA, siRNA, sgRNA, gRNA, CRISPR, Cas9,
    knockdown (KD), knockout (KO), overexpression (OE), transfection,
    transduction, viral infection, inducible expression. Keep compound
    construct names intact: "inducible shRNA-mediated LRH-1 knockdown",
    "shMLH1", "kd NR2F6", "siSCR", "shRNA_Ctrl".
  - Physical / environmental exposures: ionizing radiation,
    irradiation, UV, hypoxia, normoxia, heat shock, cold stress,
    smoking, cigarette smoke.
  - Clinical procedures used as the experimental intervention:
    surgery, resection, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, induction
    chemotherapy, chemoradiation, transplantation.

Diseases, tissues / organs / cell lines, and sample-handling lab
protocols (RNA extraction, TRIzol, Illumina library prep, FFPE
embedding, LCM, microdissection) are NOT treatments.

Coded values: a field named for a drug with a short code as its value
(e.g. `bortezomib: BR`, `dasatinib: D1`, `cisplatin: C`) indicates
that drug was applied to this sample — return the drug name from the
field label. For codes decoded by a literal legend elsewhere in this
sample's metadata (e.g. `treatment*: S, RT` + "s=surgery; RT=radiation
therapy"), return only the decoded names for codes THIS sample
actually carries.

Keep intervention names verbatim including dose/concentration tokens
when they appear attached to the drug name. Do not rewrite, expand
abbreviations, or change case.

TREATMENT — UNIVERSAL DEFINITION (must override any other guidance below): A Treatment is an in-vivo or in-vitro PERTURBATION applied to the biological sample's source organism / tissue / cell line BEFORE the molecular assay was run — drugs and their doses; genetic perturbations (shRNA, siRNA, CRISPR, KO, OE, inducible expression, transgene); irradiation; environmental exposures (hypoxia, heat shock, UV, smoke); or clinical procedures. Text that describes the MOLECULAR ASSAY ITSELF — chemical conversion of nucleic acids, hybridisation, library construction, scan / sequencing protocol, kit names, reagent names, instrument names — is NOT a Treatment, regardless of whether it appears in a field literally named `treatment_protocol`. When the only treatment-shaped text in the sample's metadata is assay-protocol text, output exactly: Not Specified.

fields.description: Sample characteristics field — usually key:value pairs.
Sample treatment/lab protocol text — may contain lab handling (not a treatment) or the actual experimental treatment. Read carefully.
Verbatim treatment / drug / intervention, or 'Not Specified'.
